definitions.technical: A maximum percentage of total reserves that an issuer may hold at a single institution. Option B in the proposed rule sets this cap at 40%, limiting exposure to any single custodian's failure.
definitions.lay: A rule that limits how much of a stablecoin issuer's reserves can be kept at any one bank. Under one proposed approach, no more than 40% of reserves could be at a single institution, so that one bank's failure cannot wipe out the majority of an issuer's backing.
definitions.parenthetical: (a regulatory ceiling on the share of reserves held at any single custodian institution)
definitions.legal_significance: The 40% concentration cap in Option B directly addresses the SVB scenario, where Circle's 8% concentration at a single custodian was sufficient to trigger a depeg. The cap provides an auditable, enforceable bright line for the monthly attestation process.
